author: zachmueller
description: Publish your notes with sliding panes and link to others notes - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Zach Mueller
manifest.description: Publish your notes with sliding panes and link to others' notes.
manifest.name: Commonplace Notes

name: Commonplace Notes

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Deploy hooks**: Added a per-profile infra-lifecycle deploy hooks subsystem. Drop `pre-deploy-hook` / `post-deploy-hook` notes into a profile's `hooks/` folder and they run at full-stack settle points (deployment wizard, auth Lambda, password gate). Hooks receive an injected AWS handle (per-service client factories, raw credentials, and `@aws-sdk/client-*` Command namespaces) plus a typed deploy context; they run for side effects and never fail the deploy. A new per-profile "Deploy hooks" settings section provides validate and export-example buttons.
- **Open note by UID**: Added a custom `obsidian://commonplace-notes?action=open-uid&uid=...` URI that resolves a `cpn-uid` to its note and opens it in a new tab (or reveals an already-open pane), with Notices for missing/unknown/duplicate UIDs.
- **Copy code fences**: Rendered code blocks on the published site now show a copy-to-clipboard button on hover (always visible on touch), with a checkmark confirmation on copy.
- **Human-readable `~` URL type for title/slug links.** Note URLs can now use `#/~{slug}` as a more readable alternative to the internal `#/t{slug}` title-type code. Both forms resolve identically (including for `;diff=` sub-targets), and `~` is now the canonical form: inbound `t` links are automatically normalized to `~` on load, so the address bar, panel identity, and dataset all display the `~` form consistently.

### UI Polish
- Added an animated loading Notice ("Writing N notes to staging" → "✓ N notes staged") covering the previously-silent per-note write-out step during publish.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **LLM chat over your published notes** — an optional AI chat backed by a Bedrock Knowledge Base, served behind CloudFront. Publishing now generates a `kb/{uid}.md` corpus artifact from your notes, and a chat widget mounts directly on the published site.

### Security & Infrastructure
- Stack deploys are now idempotent (create-or-update), so re-running the deployment wizard against an existing site works cleanly.
- Release assets now ship with GitHub build-provenance attestations for cryptographic verification.

### Bug Fixes
- "Route new note" now creates and opens the new note before routing.
- Fixed the routing suggester dismissing spuriously (deferred `onClose` null-resolve).
- Routing command errors are now surfaced via centralized error handling in `runRoute`.
- Hardened async event handlers to satisfy community-plugin review (void-Promise handlers).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Routing
- **Editable routing steps** — `cpn-routing-steps` is now a plain-string list you can edit directly, replacing the previous rigid structure.
- **`ensure-uid` action** — added a first-class routing action to guarantee notes have a UID.
- **Leaner defaults** — removed the opinionated built-in routing options and example actions, leaving routing configuration up to you.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Note-routing engine** — a composable actions-and-options system for routing notes, including a new `insert-template` action kind. Routing frontmatter keys are namespaced under `cpn-routing-`.
- **Per-note style customization** — set a note's style via `cpn-style` frontmatter, define custom CSS per named style, and expose the active `noteStyle` to parsers.
- **Active-note mode in the comments panel** — backed by a shared thread cache.

### Settings
- Redesigned the settings screen with **tabbed navigation and collapsible sections**.

### UI Polish
- Pin the note's H1 title as a **sticky header** in published panels, with refinements: correct × + title wrapping, no scrolled-text peek-through, preserved metadata row, and breathing room between the title and metadata.
- Truncate overly long note titles in the locked H1 with an ellipsis and hover tooltip.
- Make the published **"Referenced by"** section collapsible.
- Add distinct styling for plain Markdown blockquotes on the published site.

### Bug Fixes
- Fix the iPhone **"no permission"** error on password-gated sites.
- Migrate the password Lambda@Edge function from inline code to an S3-hosted asset.
- Fix Obsidian community-plugin review errors (headings, styles, minAppVersion).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
- Fixed credential refresh for AWS SSO setups, so publishing works reliably when authenticating through AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Settings
- Added an **"Unlink from AWS backend"** button to the Infrastructure settings, allowing you to disconnect a vault from its associated AWS backend.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Multi-stack import auto-discovery.** The "Import existing deployment" flow now scans your AWS account (site region plus `us-east-1`) and discovers every Commonplace Notes stack — site, Cognito, password, and commenting — instead of targeting only a single stack. A new two-phase import modal presents the discovered stacks in a role-grouped, pre-checked, override-able checklist and reconstructs the full deployment state (all 5 stack roles), with `cpn:` profile-tag filtering, read-gate resolution, and OAI/OAC detection.

### Bug Fixes
- **Fixed broken shared/copied links for folder-nested notes.** Slugs containing `/` (from folder-nested notes) were leaking their tail into the URL fragment, causing phantom panels to appear in the note list when a link was copied or shared. The decorative slug now has `/` replaced with `-` before it's prepended to the fragment. The same fix was applied to the local-preview template.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Recent Comments activity panel** — a new side panel showing recent comments, backed by a recency GSI. Comment bodies render as Markdown, with an absolute refresh timestamp and one level of threaded replies.
- **Comment wikilinks** — type `[[UID]]` in a comment to get autocomplete; published comments render note-links, the composer shows committed note-links as name chips, and links in the Recent Comments panel resolve `[[UID]]` to the note's title.
- **Open notes from comments** — modifier/middle-click a note link in the comments panel to open it in a new leaf.

### Settings
- Added a **Settings button to re-sync the Cognito OAuth callback** with the current site domain.
- Surface an **Import button for any undeployed AWS profile** and expand the import modal.

### Bug Fixes & Reliability
- Surface real AWS credential errors instead of masking them, and refresh `CloudFormationManager` clients on credential change.
- Make plugin stack-destroy robust for Lambda@Edge auth stacks.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Commenting
- Fixed the comment widget never rendering on published sites
- Gated the comment composer behind authentication and assigned unique author usernames
- Added an edit/delete UI for existing comments (with underscore Markdown emphasis support)
- Added one-level threaded replies to comments
- Made commenting discoverable after a deploy

### Authentication & Access
- Replaced the HTTP Basic Auth read-gate with a branded password unlock page
- Surfaced Cognito / Google OAuth URLs after deploy
- Clarified, defaulted, and validated the auth domain prefix in the deployment wizard
- Offered reuse of existing ACM certificates in the deployment wizard

### Infrastructure & Deployment
- Added a "Destroy infrastructure" action to the Settings Danger Zone, with an opt-in "Also delete S3 data" toggle
- Fixed destroy→redeploy failing with "stack already exists" via leftover force-clean
- Added per-profile site-asset snippet injection

### UI Polish
- Laid note metadata on one line and shortened "Last updated" to "Updated"
- Parallelized stacked-note loading on published sites for faster page loads
- Removed the URL scheme dropdown from settings

### Docs & Project
- Added an onboarding README, a full docs suite, and an MIT LICENSE

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.2.0

This is a major feature release adding **authentication & read-gating**, **self-hosted comments**, **in-plugin AWS infrastructure deployment**, and substantial publishing-pipeline and settings improvements.

### Authentication & Read-Gating
- Built-in Cognito + Google auth sub-stack with two-phase deploy wired into the plugin
- Built-in password (Basic Auth) read-gate sub-stack
- Read-gating is now a mode independent of Cognito comment identity (works with password + comments)
- Configurable Auth Lambda@Edge ARN per profile, with supporting infrastructure parameter and UI
- New `/auth` CloudFront behavior added to full-stack templates

### Comments
- Self-hosted comment backend stack
- Published-site comment client with a hardened Markdown sanitizer
- Comment-stack deploy + `/comments` origin attach wired into the plugin
- New `/comments` and `/api/comments` CloudFront behaviors

### In-Plugin Infrastructure Deployment
- Deploy AWS infrastructure directly from the plugin via CloudFormation
- Auto-discover and create Route53 hosted zones in the deployment wizard
- Replaced AWS CLI shell-exec calls with the native AWS SDK v3
- Fetch AWS account ID and refresh settings after "Apply to Profile"
- "Copy All" button for deployment completion outputs
- Infrastructure deployment user documentation

### Publishing Pipeline
- User-extensible Markdown parser with overridable built-in stages
- `remark-gfm` support for tables, strikethrough, and task lists
- Table grid CSS and Obsidian callout rendering on the published site
- Section-anchor navigation: render/link-side heading slugs + SPA runtime scroll & highlight
- Obscure wikilink titles with UIDs in published raw Markdown
- Client-side diff viewer via `;diff=` URL sub-parameter
- Prior-version jump link in note metadata
- Per-note URL fragment parameters
- Migrated site-asset source into the repo (retired sibling CDK dependency)

### Settings & UI
- Refactored settings into a profile dropdown selector with grouped sections and subsection headers
- Panel drag-to-resize with width persistence
- Copy-URL stacking for note stacks
- Theme color pickers now show real defaults and reset inline
- Lowered default UID length to 8 and exposed global settings in the UI
- Manual "Push site assets" button; site-asset upload decoupled from note publishing

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed wikilink parsing dropping later links in formatted paragraphs
- Heading-links now fall back to a working note link
- Robust home-note routing via UID-based resolution + file suggest
- Fixed startup TypeErrors from `listCommands()` usage
- Fixed import button using an unsupported `prompt()`

### Tooling & Infrastructure
- Upgraded to Node 24 LTS (release workflow, `@types/node`, added `.nvmrc` + `engines` floor; bumped `actions/checkout@v7`, `actions/setup-node@v6`)
- Expanded Playwright E2E coverage (infrastructure flows, AWS SDK migration, startup log assertions)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New in 0.1.21

### New Features
- **Delete Published Note command** — new command to delete a published note, cleaning up both the S3 object and the local mapping entry
- **Panel insertion behavior** — on locally published notes, new panels now open adjacent to the source panel instead of always appending at the end

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Modify the Markdown to HTML conversion to align to the [new URL behavior](https://github.com/zachmueller/published-commonplace-notes-cdk/commits/main/?since=2026-02-25&until=2026-02-26) in the related [publish-side CDK](https://github.com/zachmueller/published-commonplace-notes-cdk)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

- Improve error handling around presence of directories

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Minor improvements to trigger logic for visual indicators to make them less excessive and more robust
- Minor improvements to re-registering dynamic commands for publish context toggling

releases.author: github-actions[bot]

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Added starting point of new function to help with onboarding existing notes in large vaults more easily. See [explainer here](https://notes.zach.nz/#p=f9a38b791b62c8261e46f179b49b81df13fe87ef) for example usage.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Minor fix for prior update to properly add `await` statement in front of the frontmatter processing call

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Added dynamically created commands for easier toggling to add/remove any publishing profile to the active note's publish contexts
- Refactored UID creation during publishing process to commit frontmatter edits later in the publishing process to (hopefully) fix persistent bug that sometimes led to the UID showing up in the published form of the note

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Refactored how `priorHash` values are derived to (hopefully) make it more robust

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Couple minor code base improvements:

- Refactor local sub-directories to organize local data under profile-specific directories
- Implement overriding title via `cpn-title` frontmatter property
- Add new Logger class to clean up logging throughout, adding togglable debugging

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Added optional ability to publish contentIndex of full notes contents to support in-browser search on the published notes site

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
